metadata.title: Summits and Conferences in Current Affairs MCQs

questions.question: Where was the United Nations Ocean Conference (UNOC) 2025 held?
questions.explanation: Earth Sciences Minister Dr. Jitendra Singh leads Indian delegation to United Nations Ocean Conference (UNOC) in France. The UN Ocean Conference 2025 is scheduled to take place in Nice, France, from June 9 to 13, 2025. It is co-hosted by France and Costa Rica. The international meet will bring together global leaders, scientists, and policymakers to discuss sustainable ocean governance.

questions.question: The Bonn Climate Change Conference is held annually under which international agreement?

options.label: C
options.text: United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change

answer.label: C
answer.text: United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change

questions.explanation: The Bonn Climate Change Conference began on June 16, 2025, in Bonn, Germany, with more than 5,000 delegates attending. It is an annual meeting under the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC), which was signed in 1992. This mid-year summit is also called the Sessions of the UNFCCC Subsidiary Bodies (SBs), and complements the yearly Conference of the Parties (COP).

questions.question: Where was the Annual Conference of Zone II of the Commonwealth Parliamentary Association (CPA) held?
questions.explanation: Recently, Lok Sabha Speaker Shri Om Birla inaugurated the Annual Conference of Zone II of the Commonwealth Parliamentary Association (CPA) India Region at Tapovan, Dharamshala, Himachal Pradesh, on 30 June 2025. Zone II includes Delhi, Haryana, Himachal Pradesh, Jammu and Kashmir, and Punjab. The conference aims to strengthen democratic institutions, share best practices, and explore new ideas for better governance and legislative work. The Commonwealth Parliamentary Association is a global group that connects parliamentarians to promote parliamentary democracy.

questions.question: Where was the fourth International Conference on Financing for Development held?
questions.explanation: The fourth International Conference on Financing for Development began in Spain, focusing on the debt crisis holding back developing nations. Sovereign debt in developing countries rose from 16% of global debt in 2010 to 30% in 2023, growing twice as fast as in developed nations. Many developing nations face a debt trap dating back to 1970s oil shocks, leading to repeated borrowing to pay earlier loans. More than half of developing nations now spend 8% or more of government revenue on interest payments, limiting funds for healthcare, education, and climate goals.

questions.question: Where was the third edition of the National Conference on Responsible Business Conduct (NCRBC) 2025 held?
questions.explanation: Recently, the third National Conference on Responsible Business Conduct (NCRBC) 2025 was inaugurated by Shri Harsh Malhotra, Minister of State for Corporate Affairs and Road Transport & Highways, at Taj Palace, New Delhi. It was organised by the Indian Institute of Corporate Affairs (IICA), the two-day event focused on “Integrating Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) for Viksit Bharat.” The event highlighted India’s shift from regulatory prosecution to trust-based corporate governance with ESG at the centre of business strategy.

questions.question: Where was the Controllers’ Conference 2025 held?
questions.explanation: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh inaugurated the Controllers’ Conference 2025 in New Delhi on 7 July. The three-day event is hosted by the Defence Accounts Department (DAD). The conference includes eight high-level business sessions on budget reform, internal audits, collaborative research, pricing, and capacity building. The theme is ‘Transforming Financial Advice, Payment, Audit, and Accounting through Defence Finance and Economics.’

questions.question: Which country is the host of 18th BRICS Summit 2026?
questions.explanation: India will Chair BRICS in 2026. BRICS nations have officially supported India’s Chairship for the 18th BRICS Summit. They also backed India’s bid to host the 33rd Conference of the Parties (COP 33) to the UNFCCC in 2028. China and Russia reaffirmed their support for India’s permanent membership in the United Nations Security Council (UNSC). BRICS recognized India’s Startup Knowledge Hub initiative for promoting innovation and entrepreneurship.

questions.question: Where was the BIMSTEC Ports Conference 2025 held?
questions.explanation: Recently, Union Minister Sarbananda Sonowal launched the two-day BIMSTEC Ports Conference in Visakhapatnam. BIMSTEC stands for Bay of Bengal Initiative for Multi-Sectoral Technical and Economic Cooperation. The conference aims to strengthen maritime connectivity and cooperation among Bay of Bengal nations. It reflects India’s Neighbourhood First Policy and its leadership in regional maritime development. The theme is Blue Economy, Innovation, and Sustainable Partnerships. Nations participating include Bangladesh, Bhutan, India, Myanmar, Nepal, Sri Lanka, and Thailand.

questions.question: Where was the 13th International AIDS Society Conference (IAS 2025) held?
questions.explanation: On 14 July 2025, the World Health Organization (WHO) released new HIV prevention guidelines at the 13th International AIDS Society (IAS) Conference. The 13th International AIDS Society (IAS) Conference was held in Kigali, Rwanda. WHO recommended lenacapavir, a long-acting antiretroviral drug, for high-risk groups and areas with high HIV infection rates. Lenacapavir (LEN), developed by Gilead Sciences, is the first capsid inhibitor approved for pre-exposure prophylaxis (PrEP) as a twice-yearly injectable. Initially approved for HIV treatment in 2022, LEN showed near-complete protection in prevention trials. The WHO called it the most effective alternative in absence of an HIV vaccine.

questions.question: Which state is the host of 28th National Conference on e-Governance (NCeG 2025)?
questions.explanation: The 28th National Conference on e-Governance (NCeG 2025) will be held on 22-23 September 2025 at Visakhapatnam, Andhra Pradesh. It is jointly organized by the Department of Administrative Reforms and Public Grievances (DARPG), Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY), and Government of Andhra Pradesh. IIM Visakhapatnam will be the knowledge partner for the conference. The theme is “Viksit Bharat: Civil Service and Digital Transformation”. The conference will showcase best practices, emerging trends, and collaboration in digital governance for public service delivery.
